He is dedicated to his job and loves his home. Everything changes when the park becomes embroiled in a controversy. A mining company finds a huge vein of gold on the border of a protected wildlife habitat, and they demand that the park be cut down and mined to extract all of the gold.

/sbin uses his computer skills to find inconsistencies in the mining company's numbers and protocols, but he is outmatched by their contacts and their colleagues among environmental regulators. The fix broker was poisoned by radioactive material from the nuclear site and he begins coughing up black blood.

/sbin is full of holes, but his computer still works. He has a usb drive but no outlet to power it up. /sbin plugs the drive into his life support and the drive shockingly starts charging. The database is also on the USB drive. 

/sbin at first fights the unexpected power, but he thinks of all of the lives that the database could save. In a slow, painful death, /sbin uploads the database to the web before dying in the abandoned mining shaft. 

The film ends with the corrupt officials being arrested, the poachers being freed and the park being saved by the death of the ranger. The film is a commentary on the importance of protecting the environment and the lengths people have to go to in order to preserve it.
